states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
Growing up in the mountains of Bergaria, Rolotia knew only chilly springs, frost-kissed autumns, and winters so bitter they killed all memory of warmth.

Now that she's made it to the beach for the first time, she's as thrilled as she is overwhelmed. The sun shines so strongly she can feel its rays brush her skin. The sea stretches from her feet to the horizon, free and untamed, nothing like the icy peaks that shadowed her childhood.

If this is summer, she never wants it to end.

states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
Rolotia loves the beach. She'd much rather race across golden sand than harsh, glinting snow. Unfortunately, sunshine and her pale northern skin don't get along well.

After that first day, which saw her going to bed a painful shade of crimson, Rolotia has come prepared. She persuaded Rizette to part with some sun-cloaking cream in exchange for a few magically extended moments with Finn. She made herself an oasis of shade with her umbrella. But what is she to do when she wants to join her friends in the water?

Perhaps a hat can keep the sun away.

states.name: Rolotia
states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
The close of her vacation has filled Rolotia with a surprising melancholy. As the Avatar of Time, she's learned well that all good things must end. Surely a summer is nothing to mourn.

Yet each wave heralds their return to war, to worry, to reality. Could she ask Chronos to keep them here forever, suspended in a perfect summer's day, never to know suffering again?

No. The Primordial of Time would never allow it. As long as they draw breath, all living things must change. That is the foremost law of time.

But as long as she keeps the memory in her heart, Rolotia's first summer need never truly end.
